title: Neotorularia tetracmoides
taxonomy: Neotorularia tetracmoides belongs to the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and morphology, which are similar to other members of the Brassicaceae family. Neotorularia tetracmoides is often placed within the tribe Brassiceae, a subgroup of the Brassicaceae family. Further classification may include its genus and species levels, which are already mentioned as Neotorularia and tetracmoides, respectively. The plant's classification is an important aspect of its taxonomy and helps in understanding its relationships with other species in the plant kingdom.

intro: Neotorularia tetracmoides, also known as Asian burnweed or fireweed, is a flowering plant native to East Asia, characterized by its distinctive, burning sensation when touched due to the presence of irritating sap. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can aid in wound healing, reducing pain and inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Neotorularia tetracmoides include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Neotorularia tetracmoides is often used to make teas, infusions, and ointments, as well as being incorporated into herbal formulas to treat various health conditions.
